ticket_counter.speaker: Narrator
ticket_counter.text: The ticket counter glass reflects old prize lights.

ticket_counter_grounded.speaker: Narrator
ticket_counter_grounded.text: The ticket counter glass reflects old prize lights.

ticket_counter_grounded.speaker: Narrator
ticket_counter_grounded.text: The prize rates are still taped up, crossed out and lowered twice.

ticket_counter_grounded.speaker: Narrator
ticket_counter_grounded.text: A strip of tickets curls like it was dropped mid-shift.

ticket_counter_uneasy.speaker: Narrator
ticket_counter_uneasy.text: The tickets twitch when you pass.

ticket_counter_uneasy.speaker: Narrator
ticket_counter_uneasy.text: The counter smells faintly of dust and warm paper.

ticket_counter_fractured.speaker: Narrator
ticket_counter_fractured.text: The ticket counter glass catches your reflection half a beat late.

ticket_counter_fractured.speaker: Narrator
ticket_counter_fractured.text: For a moment it does not move when you move.

ticket_counter_fractured.speaker: Narrator
ticket_counter_fractured.text: Then it catches up, like nothing happened.

ticket_counter_overloaded.speaker: Narrator
ticket_counter_overloaded.text: Every ticket stub points toward the Staff Door.

ticket_counter_overloaded.speaker: Narrator
ticket_counter_overloaded.text: The roll keeps counting after it runs out of numbers.

ticket_counter_restored.speaker: Narrator
ticket_counter_restored.text: It no longer tries to sell you proof.

owner_portrait_grounded.speaker: Owner Portrait
owner_portrait_grounded.text: A dusty portrait hangs above the arcade floor, angled like it used to watch the door.

owner_portrait_grounded.speaker: Owner Portrait
owner_portrait_grounded.text: The figure wears a staff shirt, not an owner's suit.

owner_portrait_grounded.speaker: Owner Portrait
owner_portrait_grounded.text: The name patch has been worn blank.

owner_portrait_grounded.speaker: Owner Portrait
owner_portrait_grounded.text: A faded sticker on the frame reads: LAST ONE OUT LOCKS UP.

owner_portrait_grounded.speaker: Owner Portrait
owner_portrait_grounded.text: The nameplate is scratched beyond reading.

owner_portrait_uneasy.speaker: Owner Portrait
owner_portrait_uneasy.text: The scratched nameplate catches the light differently now.

owner_portrait_uneasy.speaker: Owner Portrait
owner_portrait_uneasy.text: Something under the scratches is trying to surface.

owner_portrait_uneasy.speaker: Owner Portrait
owner_portrait_uneasy.text: Not yet. The signal is still too faint to hold a name.

owner_portrait_fractured.speaker: Owner Portrait
owner_portrait_fractured.text: The scratches look less random now.

owner_portrait_fractured.speaker: Owner Portrait
owner_portrait_fractured.text: Two marks seem to repeat: 0 and 4.

owner_portrait_fractured.speaker: Owner Portrait
owner_portrait_fractured.text: Someone scratched at the name like they could not stand to be the one in the frame.

owner_portrait_overloaded.speaker: Owner Portrait
owner_portrait_overloaded.text: The portrait seems to be looking at the Staff Door.

owner_portrait_overloaded.speaker: Owner Portrait
owner_portrait_overloaded.text: The eyes look tired in a way cheap paint should not be able to hold.

owner_portrait_overloaded.speaker: Owner Portrait
owner_portrait_overloaded.text: The nameplate clicks softly in its frame, like it wants to be read and dreads it.

owner_portrait_restored.speaker: Owner Portrait
owner_portrait_restored.text: It waited until you could read it.

broken_cabinet_grounded.speaker: Broken Cabinet
broken_cabinet_grounded.text: A paper sign says OUT OF ORDER.

broken_cabinet_fractured.speaker: Broken Cabinet
broken_cabinet_fractured.text: YOU USED TO KEEP ME RUNNING LONG AFTER IT MADE SENSE.

closing_checklist_fractured.speaker: Closing Checklist
closing_checklist_fractured.text: The closing shift is signed by one name, over and over, as if no one else could be asked to be here.

closing_checklist_restored.speaker: Closing Checklist
closing_checklist_restored.text: The checklist is only paper now.

closing_checklist_restored.speaker: Closing Checklist
closing_checklist_restored.text: It stops pretending a routine can hold a night together.

maintenance_note_grounded.speaker: Maintenance Note
maintenance_note_grounded.text: Most of the note is routine cleaning nonsense.

maintenance_note_grounded.speaker: Maintenance Note
maintenance_note_grounded.text: A coffee ring covers the useful part.

maintenance_note_fractured.speaker: Maintenance Note
maintenance_note_fractured.text: Staff Door reported two signals after closing.

maintenance_note_fractured.speaker: Maintenance Note
maintenance_note_fractured.text: Gus note: I do not get paid enough for doors with opinions.

maintenance_note_overloaded.speaker: Maintenance Note
maintenance_note_overloaded.text: The note buzzes in your hand.

maintenance_note_overloaded.speaker: Maintenance Note
maintenance_note_overloaded.text: The ink keeps rewriting the word DOOR.

maintenance_note_restored.speaker: Maintenance Note
maintenance_note_restored.text: The last line is readable now.

maintenance_note_restored.speaker: Maintenance Note
maintenance_note_restored.text: Gus came back to check the lock.

maintenance_note_restored.speaker: Maintenance Note
maintenance_note_restored.text: He was scared and did the job anyway.

staff_schedule_grounded.speaker: Staff Schedule
staff_schedule_grounded.text: Mr. Byte has not unlocked staff records yet.

staff_schedule_fractured.speaker: Player
staff_schedule_fractured.text: Why does reading that make my hands cold?

staff_schedule_fractured.speaker: Staff Schedule
staff_schedule_fractured.text: The redacted name is scratched hardest, as if someone could not stand to be on this list.

staff_schedule_overloaded.speaker: Staff Schedule
staff_schedule_overloaded.text: The schedule repeats the final shift.

staff_schedule_overloaded.speaker: Staff Schedule
staff_schedule_overloaded.text: The last assignment is still redacted.

staff_schedule_restored.speaker: Staff Schedule
staff_schedule_restored.text: The final shift no longer hides its shape.

staff_schedule_restored.speaker: Staff Schedule
staff_schedule_restored.text: Everyone was where they said they were.

staff_records_fractured.speaker: Staff Record
staff_records_fractured.text: Do not repeat name until signal stabilizes.

staff_records_restored.speaker: Staff Record
staff_records_restored.text: The archive stops hiding the file.

truth_filter_machine_restored.speaker: Truth Filter
truth_filter_machine_restored.text: SUBJECT NO LONGER DENIES PRIMARY RECORD.

security_tape_terminal_restored.speaker: Security Tape
security_tape_terminal_restored.text: FRAMES NOW FORM A STAFF ROUTE.

memory_echo_object_restored.speaker: Memory Echo
memory_echo_object_restored.text: The arcade stops arguing with itself.

employee_04_file_archived.speaker: Employee File
employee_04_file_archived.text: The photo is corrupted beyond recognition.

employee_04_file_restored.speaker: Employee File
employee_04_file_restored.text: The file was never about someone else.

employee_04_file_integrated.speaker: Employee File
employee_04_file_integrated.text: The regret field is no longer sealed.

prize_counter_grounded.speaker: Prize Counter
prize_counter_grounded.text: Cheap prizes watch from behind dusty glass.

prize_counter_uneasy.speaker: Prize Counter
prize_counter_uneasy.text: A blue prize has been moved to the front.

prize_counter_fractured.speaker: Prize Counter
prize_counter_fractured.text: Three labels sit loose under the glass.

prize_counter_fractured.speaker: Prize Counter
prize_counter_fractured.text: Ticket Stub. Lost Token. Blank Employee Badge.

prize_counter_restored.speaker: Prize Counter
prize_counter_restored.text: The prize labels are neatly sorted.

prize_counter_restored.speaker: Prize Counter
prize_counter_restored.text: The badge no longer looks blank to you.

front_doors.speaker: Front Doors
front_doors.text: The main doors are chained shut from the outside.

front_doors.speaker: Front Doors
front_doors.text: A closing notice is taped to the glass, facing out, as if the street still needed telling.

front_doors.speaker: Player
front_doors.text: Why can I not just leave?

front_doors.speaker: Player
front_doors.text: Something here is not finished with me yet.

front_doors_restored.speaker: Front Doors
front_doors_restored.text: The chain is still there. Somehow it matters less now.

front_doors_restored.speaker: Front Doors
front_doors_restored.text: You were never trapped in here.

front_doors_restored.speaker: Front Doors
front_doors_restored.text: You were held. There is a difference.

arcade_history.speaker: History Board
arcade_history.text: A corkboard of photos: full weekends, tournament nights, a staff that used to be larger.

arcade_history.speaker: History Board
arcade_history.text: The dates run out a few years back.

arcade_history.speaker: History Board
arcade_history.text: The most recent photos have been taken down. The pins are still there.

closing_notice.speaker: Closing Notice
closing_notice.text: NOTICE: Pixel Haven will close following final maintenance.

closing_notice.speaker: Closing Notice
closing_notice.text: The signature under it has been scratched down to the paper.

closing_notice_restored.speaker: Closing Notice
closing_notice_restored.text: The scratched-out signature is legible now, if you already know the name.

closing_notice_restored.speaker: Closing Notice
closing_notice_restored.text: It was signed by the one person who could not bear to sign it.

party_community_wall.speaker: Community Wall
party_community_wall.text: Rows of party photos: birthday hats, gap-toothed grins, prize animals bigger than the kids holding them.

party_community_wall.speaker: Community Wall
party_community_wall.text: In the corner of almost every shot, the same figure stands half in frame.

party_community_wall.speaker: Community Wall
party_community_wall.text: Never the center. Always making sure everyone else fit.

party_community_wall_restored.speaker: Community Wall
party_community_wall_restored.text: You know the half-in-frame figure now.

party_community_wall_restored.speaker: Community Wall
party_community_wall_restored.text: He kept himself at the edge so the picture would be about the kids.

party_mascot_stage.speaker: Party Stage
party_mascot_stage.text: A little stage for a mascot costume that never quite worked.

party_mascot_stage.speaker: Party Stage
party_mascot_stage.text: Kids' drawings are still taped along the front.

party_mascot_stage.speaker: Party Stage
party_mascot_stage.text: One reads THANK YOU FOR THE FREE GO, in enormous crayon.

party_mascot_stage.speaker: Party Stage
party_mascot_stage.text: It is not addressed to anyone. It did not need to be.

party_birthday_cabinet.speaker: Birthday Cabinet
party_birthday_cabinet.text: PARTY HIGH SCORE - a cheerful, forgiving little game.

party_birthday_cabinet.speaker: Birthday Cabinet
party_birthday_cabinet.text: The target score is set absurdly low.

party_birthday_cabinet.speaker: Birthday Cabinet
party_birthday_cabinet.text: Someone wanted every kid to walk away a winner.

workshop_bench.speaker: Workbench
workshop_bench.text: A workbench under a hooded lamp, tools laid out in a patient row.

workshop_bench.speaker: Workbench
workshop_bench.text: Half-built cabinets lean against the wall, each one shaped by hand.

workshop_bench.speaker: Workbench
workshop_bench.text: Whoever worked here cared more about the games than about being paid for them.

workshop_bench_restored.speaker: Workbench
workshop_bench_restored.text: The tools are exactly where you left them.

workshop_bench_restored.speaker: Workbench
workshop_bench_restored.text: Your hands remember the weight of every one.

workshop_prototype.speaker: Prototype
workshop_prototype.text: An unfinished cabinet. No coin slot cut into it yet.

workshop_prototype.speaker: Prototype
workshop_prototype.text: A note is taped over the dark screen: MAKE THIS ONE FREE.

workshop_prototype.speaker: Prototype
workshop_prototype.text: It was never finished. The arcade closed first.

workshop_prototype.speaker: Prototype
workshop_prototype.text: The kind of thing you meant to get to once the money settled. The money never settled.

workshop_spare_parts.speaker: Spare Parts
workshop_spare_parts.text: Bins of joysticks, buttons, cracked screens.

workshop_spare_parts.speaker: Spare Parts
workshop_spare_parts.text: Everything here was kept alive long past the point of sense.

workshop_spare_parts.speaker: Spare Parts
workshop_spare_parts.text: That was the whole job, really.

memory_banks.speaker: Memory Bank
memory_banks.text: Banks of old drives hum in the dark, warm to the touch.

memory_banks.speaker: Memory Bank
memory_banks.text: Each one holds a memory the arcade refused to lose.

memory_banks.speaker: Memory Bank
memory_banks.text: Faces. Voices. Closing nights. Small kindnesses nobody else recorded.

memory_core_terminal.speaker: Core Terminal
memory_core_terminal.text: WHEN THE FLOOR WENT DARK, THE SYSTEM SAVED WHAT IT COULD.

memory_core_terminal.speaker: Core Terminal
memory_core_terminal.text: IT CHOSE PEOPLE OVER PROFIT. ONE LAST TIME.

memory_sealed_drive.speaker: Sealed Drive
memory_sealed_drive.text: One drive is labeled only with a number. The rest of the label is scratched away.

memory_sealed_drive.speaker: Sealed Drive
memory_sealed_drive.text: It runs warmer than the others.

memory_sealed_drive.speaker: Sealed Drive
memory_sealed_drive.text: It has been waiting a long time to be read.

memory_sealed_drive_restored.speaker: Sealed Drive
memory_sealed_drive_restored.text: The sealed drive is open now.

memory_sealed_drive_restored.speaker: Sealed Drive
memory_sealed_drive_restored.text: It was never corrupted. It was protected.

memory_sealed_drive_restored.speaker: Sealed Drive
memory_sealed_drive_restored.text: There is a difference, and you are the difference.

restroom_mirror.speaker: Mirror
restroom_mirror.text: A cracked mirror over a dry sink.

restroom_mirror.speaker: Mirror
restroom_mirror.text: For a moment, two figures stand where only one should.

restroom_mirror.speaker: Mirror
restroom_mirror.text: One of them finishes moving a half-second after you do.

restroom_mirror.speaker: Mirror
restroom_mirror.text: When you look again, there is only you. Probably.

restroom_mirror_restored.speaker: Mirror
restroom_mirror_restored.text: The mirror shows one figure now.

restroom_mirror_restored.speaker: Mirror
restroom_mirror_restored.text: It took both of you to get back to that.

restroom_stall.speaker: Stall
restroom_stall.text: An out-of-order stall, its door ajar.

restroom_stall.speaker: Stall
restroom_stall.text: A hand-drawn HIGH SCORE list is taped inside, going back years.

restroom_stall.speaker: Stall
restroom_stall.text: Every name on the list is written in the same handwriting.

restroom_stall.speaker: Stall
restroom_stall.text: Someone kept the record alive by pretending to be a crowd.

restroom_token.speaker: Windowsill
restroom_token.text: A single arcade token sits half behind the pipe.

restroom_token.speaker: Windowsill
restroom_token.text: It fits your hand like it remembers being held.
